name: MM without (NH4)2SO4 (nitrogen-free, chemically defined) (PMC8686765)
description: MM without (NH4)2SO4 (nitrogen-free, chemically defined) — medium reported in PMC8686765, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC8686765 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Composition mapped to BiGG (mapper + salt dissociation); presence-based bounds; paper_amount retained. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: chemically defined medium (MM: 4 g/l glucose, 34 mM NaH2PO4, 64 mM K2HPO4, 20 mM (NH4)2SO4, 1 μM Fe(SO4)4, 300 μM MgSO4, 1 μM ZnCl2, 10 μM CaCl2, adapted from (Tanaka et al, 1967))
provenance.base_medium: MM (Tanaka et al 1967 defined glucose minimal medium, i.e. 'medium 63'), with (NH4)2SO4 omitted to arrest growth (nitrogen-free)

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as Tanaka, Lerner & Lin 1967 (J Bacteriol 93:642-648, PMC276489, 'medium 63')
sources_chased.how: WebFetch pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC276489/ — article is image/scan only, methods text not OCR-available; consulted well-known medium-63 composition (KH2PO4, (NH4)2SO4, MgSO4, FeSO4 only — no additional trace-metal or vitamin mix)

recuration.notes: This is the growth-arrest variant of MM: identical to the full MM but with (NH4)2SO4 removed, making it nitrogen-free ('MM without added (NH4)2SO4 and an initial OD420 of 2.0 were used for cultivation'). Element coverage in the target was already complete (no ammonium, retains sulfate from MgSO4); this curation fills in the actual salt identities and concentrations, which were null. E. coli MG1655 is a prototroph, so no vitamins are required or present. The base (Tanaka/medium 63) carries no hidden trace-metal or vitamin stock, so the paper's explicit list is the complete recipe. 0.01 mM IPTG

uncovered.name: dipotassium hydrogen phosphate
uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

uncovered.name: iron sulfate 4' in paper, presumed typo for FeSO4)
